Rehmannia glutinosa, also known as Rehsonia, is a perennial herb native to China, belonging to the Orobanchaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, providing relief from fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Rehmannia glutinosa include iridoid glycosides, such as catalpol, and phenolic compounds like protocatechuic acid and ferulic acid. In traditional Chinese medicine, Rehmannia is often used to make decoctions and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into herbal soups and patent medicines, to promote health and vitality.
